message:
[LOW] fcp-protocol: add FCPC encode↔decode byte-stability metamorphic test

Adds encode_decode_encode_is_byte_stable_across_flags_and_sizes — a
metamorphic assertion that FcpcFrame::encode is a left-inverse of decode on
all valid frames: `decode(bytes).encode() == bytes` exactly, for the
Cartesian product of representative flag sets × payload sizes (0, 1, 15, 16,
17, 256, 4096 — covering empty, single-byte, 16-byte cipher alignment
boundaries, and a large-ish payload).

This is strictly stronger than the existing seal_round_trip, which only
checks plaintext recovery after AEAD open and would not catch header
normalization drift (e.g. length recomputation inconsistencies, flag-bit
ordering changes, session_id byte-order regressions). The test also opens
the re-decoded frame to guard against the decode/AEAD split accepting bytes
the AEAD layer would later reject.

Verified via rch: cargo test -p fcp-protocol --lib encode_decode_encode →
1 passed / 454 filtered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
